title: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future
slides: Who controls India's AI when Anthropic withdraws?
Anthropic suspends new model access in India.
Local startups now face sudden supply chain gaps.
Your AI vendor may vanish without notice tomorrow.
Build redundancy or risk your next product launch.
thread_text:
Who owns your AI?

Anthropic just cut off new model access in India. Local startups are now scrambling for alternatives.

The supply chain broke.

Startups that built their product on Claude's API now face sudden gaps. No migration plan existed.

Your vendor is fragile.

Any AI provider can vanish tomorrow. Contracts don't guarantee access when the decision is political or strategic.

Dependence is a bet.

If you only integrate one model, your next launch depends on someone else's boardroom. That's not infrastructure — it's debt.

Build redundancy today.

Take your core prompt and test it on a second model this week. If it fails, you have weeks, not months, to fix.
video_script: Anthropic blocked India. Startups lost access.
No vendor is safe from sudden withdrawal.
Your product relies on a fragile pipe.
One model means one point of failure.
Test a backup model before next launch.

instagram.caption:
Anthropic just cut off Indian startups' access to its newest models, leaving companies that built products on Claude's API without a backup plan.

Local founders now face a sudden supply chain gap with no migration ready. One boardroom decision erased months of integration work.

If your content pipeline relies on a single AI provider, your next launch depends on their strategy, not yours.

Which model will you test as a fallback this week? 🔌🔄
